preview.summary: Austria's first World Cup in 28 years meets Jordan's tournament debut in a Group J opener both sides will see as winnable. Austria's experienced spine should dominate possession, while Jordan lean on counters and the runs of their AFC Asian Cup runners-up generation.
preview.homeForm: Austria won UEFA Group H ahead of Bosnia and Romania to end a 28-year absence, ranked around 24th.
preview.awayForm: Jordan reached their maiden World Cup after years of near-misses, building on a 2023 Asian Cup final run, ranked around 63rd.
preview.homeKey: Marcel Sabitzer, Dortmund midfielder driving Austria's attack.
preview.awayKey: Musa Al-Taamari, captain and creative talisman of Jordan's golden generation.
storylines: Two ends of the spectrum: Austria's return vs Jordan's debut
Rangnick's pressing system against Jordan's compact counters
Both treat this as Group J's key three-pointer with Argentina looming
preview.h2h: The sides have met only once, a previous draw with no decisive history between them.
